Title: Gene regulatory networks for lignin biosynthesis in switchgrass

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: secondary cell wall formation; biomass recalcitrance relevant to biofuel processing
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased total lignin and S/G ratio and reduced plant height; knockdown reduced expression of several lignin genes but caused no obvious lignin phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: positive activator of lignin biosynthesis and secondary wall formation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: up-regulates most lignin biosynthesis genes including F5H; RNA-seq also showed increased expression of SWN1, SWN2, PvMYB46, MYB42/85, MYB32, MYB103 and SND2
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: lignin biosynthesis and broader secondary wall formation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: F5H; COMT; most lignin biosynthesis genes except C3'H/C3OH in some assays
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: evidence for feedback regulation in the NAC-MYB secondary wall network
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: more highly expressed in stem than in leaf blade and leaf sheath; expressed in multiple cell types in stem cross sections
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: ZmUbi1 constitutive promoter for overexpression/RNAi constructs
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; RNAi knockdown; qRT-PCR; in situ hybridization; lignin staining; acetyl bromide lignin assay; thioacidolysis; RNA-seq; promoter transactivation assay
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate target to increase lignin and alter S/G ratio, though growth penalty limits deployment for biofuel feedstocks
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: microarray co-expression analysis; RNA-seq SRA PRJNA480162
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvMYB58/63A is stem-enriched and associated with secondary wall-forming tissues. 2. Overexpression increased lignin content (AcBr and thioacidolysis), increased S/G ratio, and induced most lignin pathway genes including F5H. 3. It functions as a positive regulator of secondary wall formation but causes dwarfing when overexpressed.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct activation was confirmed for PvCOMT promoter, but PvF5H promoter was not directly activated in protoplast assay, suggesting indirect regulation or cofactor requirement in planta.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: secondary cell wall formation; biomass recalcitrance relevant to biofuel processing
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased total lignin and S/G ratio and reduced plant height; RNAi reduced some lignin-gene expression but no strong opposite lignin phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates F5H and other lignin biosynthesis genes; directly activates COMT and weakly activates F5H promoters
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: lignin biosynthesis and secondary wall formation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: F5H; COMT; multiple lignin biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: part of NAC-MYB transcriptional network with possible feedback relationships
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highest in stems; expressed throughout stem including vascular bundles and epidermal cells, preferentially in xylem vessels
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: ZmUbi1 constitutive promoter for overexpression/RNAi constructs
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; RNAi knockdown; qRT-PCR; in situ hybridization; lignin staining; acetyl bromide lignin assay; thioacidolysis; RNA-seq; promoter transactivation assay
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful for raising lignin and S-unit enrichment, but growth penalty reduces value for bioenergy improvement
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: microarray co-expression analysis; RNA-seq SRA PRJNA480162
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvMYB42/85A is stem-preferential and enriched in xylem-related tissues. 2. Overexpression increased lignin content, S/G ratio and lignin gene expression including F5H. 3. It directly transactivates PvCOMT and weakly PvF5H promoters, supporting a positive role in lignin regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RNA-seq suggested target preference differences relative to PvMYB58/63A; significant activation in tillers excluded some genes such as PAL, C3'H and CCoAOMT.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: secondary wall formation; biomass recalcitrance relevant to biofuel processing
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: negative regulator of lignification; previously reported overexpression phenotype used here in RNA-seq and transactivation analyses
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: secondary wall repressor acting oppositely to PvMYB58/63A
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: down-regulates lignin biosynthesis genes broadly in RNA-seq; weakly represses PvCOMT promoter; alters F5H expression in planta
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: repression of lignin biosynthesis and associated secondary wall pathways
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: suggested feedback regulation within NAC-MYB network
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: ZmUbi1 constitutive promoter for overexpression constructs
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: partially independent of NAC-MYB46-MYB cascade according to co-expression interpretation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: previous overexpression study; RNA-seq; promoter transactivation assay
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: severe phenotype reported previously; broad down-regulation of flavonoid and cellulose pathways in internodes
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: potential engineering target to reduce lignin for improved saccharification, but pleiotropy should be considered
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvMYB4 acts as a negative regulator of lignin biosynthesis. 2. RNA-seq showed broad opposite effects relative to PvMYB58/63A on lignin and other cell-wall-related genes. 3. It weakly repressed the PvCOMT promoter and likely modulates F5H indirectly in planta.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Experimental phenotype details are referenced from prior publication rather than fully repeated in this paper.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: controls lignification pattern in stem secondary walls
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: down-regulation caused enhanced lignification in epidermal cells and vascular bundles and increased pith cell wall thickness, with reduced plant growth and disrupted xylem/tracheids reported previously
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: repressor of lignification in pith parenchyma/secondary wall development
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: not a direct regulator of lignin pathway genes; co-expressed with PvSWN1 and PvSWN2B; candidate downstream targets from integrated analysis include bZIP63 homolog Pavir.Cb00258, bZIP44 homolog Pavir.Fb01406, and WRKY41 homologs Pavir.Bb02088 and Pavir.J16737
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: spatial control of secondary wall lignification
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PvSWN1; PvSWN2B co-expression association; candidate targets Pavir.Cb00258; Pavir.Fb01406; Pavir.Bb02088; Pavir.J16737
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highest transcript abundance in stems; in situ signal highest in pith parenchyma cells
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: dominant repressor transgenics; qRT-PCR; in situ hybridization; stem anatomy; phloroglucinol staining; RNA-seq plus co-expression integration
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: reduced plant growth; disruption of xylem vessels and tracheids
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: possible target to alter spatial lignification, but vascular defects and growth penalties are major cautions
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvWRKY12 is stem- and pith-enriched. 2. Down-regulation enhances pith and stem lignification and thickens pith cell walls. 3. It likely represses secondary wall formation indirectly rather than directly regulating lignin pathway genes.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike Arabidopsis reports, no significant changes in SWN expression were observed in WRKY12-DR lines in this study.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: master regulation of secondary wall biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RNAi caused no obvious changes in growth, lignin content or stem structure, except slightly increased S/G ratio in two lines
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: putative master regulator but not decisive alone in this study due to redundancy
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: decreased PvSWN2 expression did not clearly alter expression of PvSWN1, PvMYB4, PvMYB46, PvMYB58/63, PvMYB42/85 or F5H
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation (inferred for SWN family); not directly established here
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: ZmUbi1 constitutive promoter for RNAi constructs
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown; qRT-PCR; lignin assays; stem structural analysis
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: no major phenotype observed under tested conditions
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: limited as single-gene target because functional redundancy likely masks effects; may require stacking with related NAC regulators
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvSWN2A/B are co-expressed with lignin biosynthesis genes. 2. RNAi knockdown produced little effect on lignin or growth. 3. Functional redundancy with other SWNs likely limits detectable phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper treats PvSWN2A and PvSWN2B together functionally in RNAi lines.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAC-related secondary wall regulator; SND2 clade
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RNAi reduced expression of MYB103, CESA4 and CESA9; reduced hemicellulose content in one line and lowered galactose/glucose proportions in hemicellulose fraction, with no significant lignin or growth changes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: positive regulator of secondary wall cellulose/hemicellulose biosynthesis rather than lignin
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: promotes expression of MYB103; CESA4; CESA9
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: secondary wall cellulose and hemicellulose biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: genes in SND2 clades correlated with multiple lignin biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: ZmUbi1 constitutive promoter for RNAi constructs
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown; qRT-PCR; cell wall polysaccharide analysis
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: no significant growth penalty observed in reported lines
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate to tune secondary wall polysaccharide composition with modest visible penalty
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvSND2 knockdown lowered expression of cellulose/secondary wall genes MYB103, CESA4 and CESA9. 2. It affected hemicellulose quantity/composition more than lignin. 3. PvSND2 likely functions in cellulose/hemicellulose biosynthesis during secondary wall formation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although identified via lignin-centered co-expression, functional effect was mainly on polysaccharide wall components.
